{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient is a 36-week gestation female presenting with hypertension, visual blurring, and headache, with blood pressure readings of 180/120 mm Hg and 174/110 mm Hg. These symptoms and blood pressure readings indicate severe hypertension, which is a sign of preeclampsia. According to the guidelines, severe hypertension in pregnancy should be managed immediately to prevent end-organ damage. The patient should be admitted for close monitoring and treatment. Magnesium sulfate is indicated for seizure prophylaxis in preeclampsia, and delivery is recommended as soon as possible to prevent maternal and fetal complications. Options A, B, and D do not include magnesium sulfate and/or do not address the urgency of the situation by admitting the patient and starting antihypertensives. Option C is the most appropriate as it includes admission, antihypertensives, magnesium sulfate, and termination of pregnancy.", "answer_choice": "C"}
